channel.title: Per page discussions (new posts)
channel.description: Posts in the forum category "Per page discussions" - This category groups discussions related to particular pages within this site.

item.title: SCP-5692:
item.content:encoded:
Very much reminds me of Star Wars bars, MIB, Valerian and Laureline (french comic). The good parts of all of these, anyhow.

I wish more articles of this quality was written.

item.title: SCP-5104:
item.content:encoded:
Love the energy. I, personally, am working on a cute-but-terrifying scp, and just really enjoyed reading this. 5104-1 is adorable, and the chiropractor is hilariously unaware of the situation he's in.

The nonchalant "eh, giant bone snake hasn't killed anyone, let's give it what it wants" from the foundation staff if also great. I love the idea that they're so used to world-ending horrors that when something just wants its spine set, they're willing to just accept it and throw a dude in there with an entirely unknown anomaly.

Since the wiki doesn't have red shapes, you get 1 little cross with a number 1 after it, and a spot on my list of favorite scps.

item.title: Crying Over Coffee:
item.content:encoded:
While this was heartbreaking… my suspension of disbelief was broken by something very dumb.
There are no Waffle House locations in Wisconsin.

item.title: SCP-5547: Re: Staff Post - Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded: ii

item.title: SCP-5127: Re: Staff Post- Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Third senior staff voting for deletion and confirming that this page meets deletion criteria.

☑ ☑ ☑

item.title: SCP-5547: Staff Post - Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Beginning deletion vote at -11.

If you are not the author and you want to rewrite this article, you may reply to this post asking for the opportunity to do so. Please obtain permission from the author (or the Rewrite Team if this article is older than 6 months) and make sure you copy the page source to your sandbox. Please do not reply to this post for any other reason unless you are staff.

item.title: The Battle of Baikal:
item.content:encoded:
The story is really nice (no not only because I'm biased and that I think that the CotBG is the best GoI imo), and apparently I'm to dumb and can't translate the "real" Name of Bumaro into Linear B. Could ya give me a lil hint ;D

Re: Okay maybe I have it now, is it
+ show block
– hide block

Hephaestus cause he's a smith and u know Bumaro smithes a lot I guess

item.title: The Mekhanics of La Rue Part Two:
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad to finally see the continuation of Josephine and Benjamin's journey. Really good stuff Blue.

+1

item.title: SCP-5134: Re: Staff Post - Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Second senior staff voting for deletion and confirming that this page meets deletion criteria.

/ /

item.title: The Moloch Mentality: Re: Mind blown
SCP-5134: Re: If this needs to be Top Secret, then...
item.content:encoded:
I've got kind of this idea in my head that it was created as a guest book for some sort of anomalous bed and breakfast, and it records not just the reader's name, but everything they did since they had their last REM cycle, and continues recording until either they have another, or they die. Maybe throw in a comment about how a majority of the book is taken up by the story of a person who was on anxiety or anti-depressant medication that stopped them from going into REM sleep?

I like the idea of it simply recording things, rather than compelling you to read.

The foundation could even have ways of getting the information out by having a D-class with an extremely common name wakeup and immediately begin photocopying pages, and then go back to sleep before being allowed to interact with any foundation personnel?

There's a lot you could do with the idea. It's a shame the article was so sparse and didn't go into any of the possibilities.

item.title: SCP-5134: Staff Post - Deletion Vote
item.content:encoded:
Beginning deletion vote at -12.

If you are not the author and you want to rewrite this article, you may reply to this post asking for the opportunity to do so. Please obtain permission from the author (or the Rewrite Team if this article is older than 6 months) and make sure you copy the page source to your sandbox. Please do not reply to this post for any other reason unless you are staff.

item.title: SCP-5134: If this needs to be Top Secret, then...
item.content:encoded: Might I suggest the book revealing personal data about the opener — the kind of information a skilled private detective would uncover doing the same amount of field work? (One hour reading about yourself = a one-hour hunt for clues.) The book "resets" when it changes hands, so it couldn't be used to grab someone else's personal secrets directly. However, another person could persuade/compel the holder to read the contents out loud while a third person took notes. With this much potential for blackmail, no wonder the Foundation wants SCP-5134 locked away from lower-clearance personnel!

item.title: The Mekhanics of La Rue Part Two:
item.content:encoded:
Been waiting a long time for this, and I'm glad how it turned out!

+1

item.title: Log Of Anomalous Items, Vol III:
item.content:encoded: Added the calculator that always answers 666.

item.title: SCP-4273:
item.content:encoded:
Overall, the core anomaly and general narrative are far too dull and short-lived to be enjoyable. The anomalous attributes exhibited by SCP-4273 aren't detailed particularly well, and from what I can get out of the description, what it does do isn't very interesting; something more of an AI (anomalous item) than something that would be given its own SCP № designation.

As for the narrative, it's quite bland in all honesty. An individual in power binding their soul to an object to maintain power even after death isn't very original; bordering on a cliché at this point. Furthermore, even if the narrative was something of grand uniqueness and intrigue, the article itself is far too short to convey much meaning and makes it hard for me to have any notable feelings for what's going on here.
-1

item.title: SCP-4273:
item.content:encoded: Images are public domain, will provide more detailed info tonight. Thanks for reading.

item.title: The Mekhanics of La Rue Part Two:
item.content:encoded:
There were a lot of improvements since I last looked at this piece. The first half definitely felt a lot better and did a good job at drawing me in. The action scene felt more energetic like an action scene should.

Despite what I said, though, the second half still fell a little short and had a good deal of stiffness, but the enjoyment level trumps it just enough for me to give an upvote. It's not my favorite piece I ever read, but it's always good to see dedication.

+1

item.title: The Moloch Mentality: Re: Mind blown
item.content:encoded: I mean, seriously though, this does somewhat have that movie vibe, and now that I realized it I think I love the series even more.

item.title: SCP-5134:
item.content:encoded:
Interesting concept, but there isn't really that much there that makes it an SCP. I believe there is a list of anomalous items that don't have enough properties to really be a full-blown SCP article. It could be pretty good over there. I like the idea, but the execution could use some touching-up.

I also feel like the containment procedures are extremely overdone. If it can be safely kept in a box, the foundation will put it in a box. or, more accurately, a secured locker. If the pedestal isn't necessary for containment, it shouldn't be there. If it is, explain why. The clinical tone could use quite a bit of work too.

If you really want this to be an SCP, I recommend working on it to try to make it better for being used for storytelling purposes.

I've also realized that this SCP's clearance requirements are way too high given it's anomalous abilities. The O-5 have much bigger fish to fry than someone wanting a book to have their name in it. And level-4 clearance requirements are also too high. Level 4-clearance is considered Top-Secret. This is something that you really don't want the most important people in the foundation touching, as it gives their enemies a nice little list of everyone important in the foundation and their names.
